scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: pre-engagement_baseline — both parties are at the starting line with no relational signals yet visible; this is the blank slate moment before substantive interaction

emerging polarization around logistics-as-proxy — Party A using child welfare/logistics as gatekeeping rationale, Party B experiencing this as dismissal of parental legitimacy. Both parties advocating positions but underlying interests suggest potential compatibility if reframed. Risk of escalation if identity/respect dimension not surfaced and validated.

emerging collaborative problem-solving — parties have moved from positional defense to conditional openness around phased trial concept, but underlying trust deficit remains; both need assurance their core concerns will be operationalized, not dismissed

notes: Turn 1 presents no behavioral data — parties have not yet spoken or acted
Baseline assumptions: parties arrive with natural caution at process start
Any perception at this stage is structural rather than observational
First substantive signals will emerge after Solomon's opening and parties' initial responses
Party A has framed concerns around child welfare rather than personal preference
Language suggests experienced difficulties ('becomes dysregulated', 'irregular exchanges')
No signals yet from Party B - critical to hear their perspective before proceeding
Risk of implicit blame in Party A's framing ('until... reliable') - may trigger defensiveness
Both parties are using reasonable-sounding frames that mask deeper concerns
Party A's consistency focus may blend legitimate child needs with schedule control
Party B's frustration about 'secondary parent' status is identity-based, not just schedule-based
The term 'block time' suggests Party B perceives bad faith
Neither party has yet named what success would actually look like for them
Significant shift from turns 2-4: both parties now using 'if/then' language rather than absolute positions
Both acknowledge logistics as shared constraint rather than character indictment
Risk: premature option work could collapse this fragile alignment if conditions aren't adequately specified first
Party A needs reliability operationalized; Party B needs timeline operationalized
